blocks:
Ayahs

ٱقْرَأْ بِٱسْمِ رَبِّكَ ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَ (۱)
Read in the Name of your Lord who created; (1)
خَلَقَ ٱلْإِنسَـٰنَ مِنْ عَلَقٍ (۲)
created man from a clinging mass. (2)
ٱقْرَأْ وَرَبُّكَ ٱلْأَكْرَمُ (۳)
Read, and your Lord is the most generous, (3)
ٱلَّذِى عَلَّمَ بِٱلْقَلَمِ (٤)
who taught by the pen, (4)
عَلَّمَ ٱلْإِنسَـٰنَ مَا لَمْ يَعْلَمْ (٥)
taught man what he did not know. (5)

Key Points

The Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: The first time Gabriel appeared to me, he said: Read. I said: I cannot. After pressing me three times, I found that I could read. (16) We read in Tafsir Novin (Modern Commentary): Where the issue of human creation is considered, Allah calls Himself Karim (Generous): «ما غرّك بربّك الكریم الّذى خلقك» (17) But where the topic of reading, learning, and the pen is raised, He calls Himself Akram (Most Generous). «اقرأ و ربّك الاكرم الّذى علّم بالقلم» It is among the glories of Islam that it began its mission with recitation, knowledge, and the pen, and the first command from Allah to His Prophet was a cultural command. The reading of the tablet that was opened before the Prophet for the first time was organized and written. The command Iqra' (Read) contains the point that what will be revealed to you is meant to be read, not just known. Several meanings have been given for the word 'alaq: A) Clinging/Adhesive, meaning that Adam was created from clinging clay, or that the male sperm clings to the female ovum like a leech. B) Meaning thick, clotted blood, which is the commonly discussed meaning. He Who creates man from clotted blood commands the unlettered person to read (Iqra'). The beginning of seeking knowledge must be in the Name of God. «اقرء باسم ربّك» Graduates must also be in His path. «فاذا فرغت فانصب و الى ربّك فارغب»

16) Tafsir Namuneh. 17) Al-Infitar, 6 - 7.

Messages

1- The recitation of the Quran must begin with the Name of Allah. «اقرء باسم ربّك»

2- The first command of Islam is a cultural command. «اقرء»

3- Reading must have a divine orientation. «اقرء باسم ربّك»

4- Reading the Quran is a means of growth. «اقرء و ربّك»

5- Divine nurturing is both material, «ربك الّذى خلق - خلق الانسان من علق», and spiritual. «ربّك الاكرم - الّذى علّم بالقلم»

6- The Lord (Rabb) is the same as the Creator. (Therefore, the statement of the polytheists who accepted that God was the Creator but considered idols to be the Lord (Rabb) is false.) «ربّك الّذى خلق»

7- Stating the creation of man from clotted blood is both a scientific prediction and a way to educate man so that he knows his origin and does not fall into arrogance. «خلق الانسان من علق»

8- The creation of all existence is on one side «الّذى خلق» and the creation of man is on another side. «خلق الانسان من علق»

9- The primary teacher of man is Allah. «علّم الانسان ما لم یعلم»

10- The pen is the best means for transmitting knowledge. «علّم بالقلم»

11- Divine revelation is the source of human sciences. «علّم الانسان ما لم یعلم»

12- Allah's Creatorship, Lordship, Generosity, and role as Teacher necessitate the recitation of His Book. «اقرء باسم ربّك... خلق... الاكرم... علّم...»

13- Allah carries out His works through means (causes). «علّم بالقلم»

14- Freedom from ignorance through the use of the pen is a manifestation of His Generosity and Lordship, and writing is a desirable art encouraged by Islam. «علّم بالقلم»

15- Allah nurtures both the body and the soul of man. (The word Rabbik (your Lord) appears between the two words Khalaq (created) and Iqra' (read).) «اقرء باسم ربّك الّذى خلق»

Explanations

Portrait of Surah Alaq. This Surah has nineteen verses and was revealed in Mecca. The name of the Surah is taken from the second verse, which refers to the creation of man from 'alaq (clotted blood). This Surah is the first Surah revealed to the Prophet, and the sequence of its verses is so interconnected that it can be said the entire Surah was revealed at once. (15) This Surah, like Surahs Sajdah, Fussilat, and Najm, contains a mandatory prostration (Sajdah Wajib), which, according to the Shia belief, should not be recited during prayer, and reciting them while in the state of menstruation (hayd) or major ritual impurity (janabah) is forbidden.

15) Tafsir Al-Mizan.

Ayahs

كَلَّآ إِنَّ ٱلْإِنسَـٰنَ لَيَطْغَىٰٓ (٦)
Indeed man becomes rebellious (6)
أَن رَّءَاهُ ٱسْتَغْنَىٰٓ (۷)
when he considers himself without need. (7)
إِنَّ إِلَىٰ رَبِّكَ ٱلرُّجْعَىٰٓ (۸)
Indeed to your Lord is the return. (8)
أَرَءَيْتَ ٱلَّذِى يَنْهَىٰ (۹)
Tell me, he who forbids (9)
عَبْدًا إِذَا صَلَّىٰٓ (۱٠)
a servant when he prays, (10)
أَرَءَيْتَ إِن كَانَ عَلَى ٱلْهُدَىٰٓ (۱۱)
tell me, should he be on [true] guidance, (11)
أَوْ أَمَرَ بِٱلتَّقْوَىٰٓ (۱۲)
or bid [others] to Godwariness, (12)
أَرَءَيْتَ إِن كَذَّبَ وَتَوَلَّىٰٓ (۱۳)
tell me, should he call him a liar and turn away (13)
أَلَمْ يَعْلَم بِأَنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَرَىٰ (۱٤)
—does he not know that Allah sees [him]? (14)

Key Points

The word "كلاّ" (Kallā) is either meant to negate the preceding statement, meaning it is not the case that man remembers his past—that Allah created him and taught him, and thus he should be His servant—but rather, instead of servitude, he rebels and prevents others from servitude as well. And it is possible that the word "كلاّ" (Kallā) means "truly" or "certainly," used for emphasis. (18)

If a person lacks capacity (or inner strength), either wealth makes him arrogant, just as Qarun said: «انّما اوتیته على علم عندى» (I have been given this only because of knowledge I possess) (19); or power makes him arrogant, just as Pharaoh said: «الیس لى ملك مصر» (Is not the dominion of Egypt mine?) (20); or knowledge makes him arrogant, just as Bal'am Bā'ūrā suffered from it: «آتیناه آیاتنا فانسلخ منها» (We gave him Our signs, but he slipped away from them). (21) But if there is capacity, even if all three (wealth, power, and knowledge) are combined in one person, such as Prophets Joseph (Yūsuf) and Solomon (Sulaymān), he does not become arrogant, because he attributes everything to Allah, not to himself. Just as Prophet Solomon said: «هذا من فضل ربّى» (This is by the grace of my Lord) (22), and Prophet Joseph said: «ربّ قد آتیتنى من الملك و علّمتنى من تأویل الاحادیث» (My Lord, You have given me [a share] of the sovereignty and taught me the interpretation of dreams). (23) Yes, the danger lies where man looks to himself, not to God. (24) «أن رآه استغنى»

The tyrant is neither a servant of God, nor does he recognize divine commands, nor does he accept reasoning, nor does he listen to the call of conscience or the cries of the oppressed.

The intent behind the servant who prays, «عبداً اذا صلّى» (a servant when he prays), as is evident from the final verses of the Surah, is the Messenger of Allah, because in those verses, He forbids the Prophet from obeying that person and invites him to prostration (Sujud) and proximity (Qurb). Since these verses are among the first verses revealed to the Prophet, he was performing prayer even before attaining the status of prophethood (Risalah). (25)

We read in the narrations (Ahadith): Abu Jahl asked his companions: Does the Prophet place his face on the ground for prostration among you? They said: Yes. He said: I swear by that which we swear by, if I see him in such a state, I will crush his neck with my foot. When he realized the Prophet was praying, he decided to kick the Prophet's blessed head while He was in prostration. As soon as he approached, he retreated in a strange state and told the polytheists: When I got close to Him, I saw a trench of fire before me. (26)

Although the intent of «عبداً» (a servant) is the Prophet Himself, its indefinite form (Nakra) is used for magnification and reverence. «عبدًا اذا صلّى»

18) Tafsir Namūneh. 19) Al-Qasas, 78. 20) Az-Zukhruf, 51. 21) Al-A'rāf, 175. 22) An-Naml, 40. 23) Yūsuf, 101. 24) Tafsir Novin. 25) Tafsir Al-Mīzān. 26) Tafasir Majma' al-Bayān and Namūneh.

Messages

  1. Man is ungrateful. We created him and taught him everything he did not know. But he rebels against his Lord. «كلاّ انّ انسان لیطغى»
  2. Man resorts to transgression because he wrongly imagines himself to be self-sufficient. «لیطغى ان رآه استغنى»
  3. Preventing prayer is the clearest type of evil (Munkarāt). «ینهى عبداً اذا صلّى»
  4. Even if they consider the possibility of righteousness, they should not treat the Prophet in this manner. «ان كان على الهُدى»
  5. Man lacks capacity, and the illusion of self-sufficiency intoxicates him. «لیطغى ان رآه استغنى»
  6. Knowledge alone is not sufficient. Although We gave man knowledge, he still transgresses. «علّم الانسان ما لم یعلم - انّ الانسان لیطغى»
  7. Being rich (Ghanī) is perfection, but seeing oneself as rich (self-sufficient) is the ground for errors. «راه استغنى»
  8. Belief in the Resurrection (Ma'ād) prevents transgression. «انّ الى ربّك الرجعى»
  9. We leave behind everything we possess and depart; let us not imagine ourselves to be self-sufficient. «انّ الى ربّك الرجعى»
  10. Man and existence are purposeful and directed toward God. «انّ الى ربّك الرجعى»
  11. Tyrants (Tāghūtiyān) are opposed to prayer. «لیطغى - ینهى عبدًا اذا صلّى» (Improper behavior is censured when it is continuous.)
  12. The sign of servitude to Allah is prayer. «عبدًا اذا صلّى»
  13. Tyrants are displeased with servitude to God. (Not with indifferent individuals or those who are servants of their own desires or the desires of others). «ینهى عبدًا اذا صلّى»
  14. Name and identity are not important; actions are important. (The name of the prohibitor and the one praying are not mentioned.) «ینهى - صلّى»
  15. It is fitting that the one who commands piety (Taqwā) should himself be among the rightly guided. «كان على الهُدى او امر بالتّقوى»
  16. The root of transgression is two things: first, that he sees himself as self-sufficient. «رآه استغنى» Second, that he does not see God and imagines that God does not see him either. «ألم یعلم بانّ اللّه یرى»

Ayahs

كَلَّا لَئِن لَّمْ يَنتَهِ لَنَسْفَعًۢا بِٱلنَّاصِيَةِ (۱٥)
No indeed! If he does not cease, We shall seize him by the forelock, (15)
نَاصِيَةٍ كَـٰذِبَةٍ خَاطِئَةٍ (۱٦)
a lying, sinful forelock! (16)
فَلْيَدْعُ نَادِيَهُۥ (۱۷)
Then let him call out his gang! (17)
سَنَدْعُ ٱلزَّبَانِيَةَ (۱۸)
We [too] shall call the keepers of hell. (18)
كَلَّا لَا تُطِعْهُ وَٱسْجُدْ وَٱقْتَرِب ۩ (۱۹)
No indeed! Do not obey him, but prostrate and draw near [to Allah]! (19)

Key Points

«نسفعاً» (Nasfa'an) is a verb, and the nūn of emphasis (nūn al-tawkid) at its end is written as a tanwīn. This word comes from the root nasfa‘ meaning to seize, to drag violently, and to slap the face. «ناصیة» (Nāsiyah) means the hair at the front of the forehead, which is seized and dragged for the purpose of humiliation. When Sūrat ar-Raḥmān was revealed, the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله asked: Who will recite it for the chiefs of Quraysh? The companions remained silent due to the harsh and violent treatment by the chiefs of Quraysh, but ‘Abdullāh ibn Mas‘ūd, who had a weak physique, accepted. As soon as he saw the leaders near the Ka‘bah, he recited the verses. Abū Jahl slapped him so hard that blood flowed from his ear. He came weeping to the Prophet, and the حضرت became saddened. In the Battle of Badr, ‘Abdullāh ibn Mas‘ūd saw Abū Jahl among the slain polytheists in his final moments and sat upon his chest. Abū Jahl said, Do you know where you are sitting?! He replied, «الاسلام یعلو و لا یُعلى علیه» (Islam is exalted and nothing is exalted above it). Abū Jahl said, Tell the Prophet: even in this state, he is the most hateful person to me. The حضرت said: The Pharaoh of Moses believed in the final moment, but my Pharaoh did not believe even in the final moment. Ibn Mas‘ūd severed Abū Jahl’s head from his body, seized his forelock, and dragged it to the Prophet, and the verse «لنسفعاً بالنّاصیة» (We will surely drag him by the forelock) was fulfilled in this very world. (27) The word «نادى» (Nādī) refers to a public assembly or a place of recreation. «زبانیة» (Zabāniyah) is the plural of zabīnah, meaning an officer or agent. Imam Ali عليه السلام says: The fruit of knowledge is the servitude of Allah. The beginning of this Sūrah was the command to read and teach by the pen, and its end is prostration (sujūd) and drawing near (taqarrub). That is, beneficial knowledge is the knowledge that leads us to Allah. Of course, the path of proximity to Allah includes all deeds performed with the intention of seeking proximity (qurbah), but prostration is the best path to proximity. The word «كلاّ» (Kallā) appears three times in this short Sūrah. This means one must avoid baseless illusions and conjectures. Imam Riḍā عليه السلام, citing the verse «و اسجد و اقترب» (And prostrate and draw near), said: The closest state of a human being to Allah is the state of prostration. (28)

27) Tafsīr-i Namūneh, quoting Tafsīr Kabīr. 28) Tafsīr Nūr al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- The path of repentance is open to everyone, but if they do not repent and desist, they will face punishment. «لئن لم ینته لنسفعاً»

2- Threatening is necessary against oppressors. «لنسفعًا بالناصیة»

3- Lying and falsehood are the root of other errors and transgressions. «كاذبة خاطبة»

4- The support structure for the opponents' harmful actions consists of secret assemblies. «ینهى عبدا اذا صلى - فلیدع نادیه»

5- Although the Prophet’s opponents were organized and structured, they achieved nothing. «فلیدع نادیه»

6- The reward for throwing clods of earth is a stone (i.e., retaliation is severe). «فلیدع نادیه - سندع الزّبانیة»

7- The power of Allah prevails over all conspiracies. «سندع الزبانیة»

8- In the face of prohibition and prevention by others, you must insist on performing acts of worship. «ینهى عبدًا اذا صلّى - واسجد»

9- First disavowal, then worship. «لاتطعه واسجد» (Do not follow the Abū Jahls who forbid prayer, and engage in worship and prostration.)

10- Prostration is the best means of drawing near to Allah. «واسجد واقترب»
